purpose: Establish criteria-free baseline matching cycle27 config (section_threshold=40, custody audit-only)
command: scripts/run_phase0_smoke.ps1 -RunLabel phase0_baseline_match -EnvFile .env.phase0_baseline_match -Concurrency 16
notes: Criteria-free baseline. 7.3pp gap from criteria-aided cycle27 (85.2%). Confirms rubric criteria were the dominant factor.

purpose: Test if completeness criteria (active) + longer summaries (2000 chars) close criteria-free gap
command: scripts/run_phase0_smoke.ps1 -RunLabel phase0_synthesis_fix -EnvFile .env.phase0_synthesis_fix -Concurrency 16
notes: Wash: +0.4pp vs baseline-match. Synthesis-side fixes cannot close the 7pp gap. Completeness criteria don't correlate with score. Codex directed: abandon synthesis-side, implement Domain Lens.

purpose: Domain lens: second seed pass generating domain-specific issue scaffolding to close criteria-free gap
command: scripts/run_phase0_smoke.ps1 -RunLabel phase0_domain_lens -EnvFile .env.phase0_domain_lens -Concurrency 16
notes: NEGATIVE RESULT. Go gate FAILS all 4 criteria. Overall -1.8pp regression (77.1%->75.3%). Top-10 regressed -5.4pp. Even rich-lens tasks regressed -2.0pp avg. Lens dilutes extraction focus on high-performing tasks. 31% lens generation failure (15/48 empty). Catastrophic regressions: extract-psa-key-terms -32pp, extract-assets -34pp, draft-subscription -28pp. Domain lens approach is net harmful in current form.

purpose: First A/B of new target-closing loop (src/loop) vs W13 on 5-task probe (2 worst, 2 mid, 1 best)
command: SWARM_ARCH=loop python -m src.cli batch benchmarks/manifests/loop_compare_5.json -o results/loop_compare5 -j 5
notes: Disasters +34/+52pp; extraction tasks -27pp (packet starvation + chunk tail loss)

purpose: Completeness fixes: 48 claims/packet, 400K packet budget, 40K chunks, exhaustive-term prompt
command: SWARM_ARCH=loop python -m src.cli batch benchmarks/manifests/loop_compare_5.json -o results/loop_compare5_v2 -j 5
notes: insurance 55->76, NPDES 56->69, petition 69->84, reg-stmt 46->51; remaining NPDES gap = requirements in engagement email never extracted

purpose: True 48-task A/B: loop vs W13 on identical manifest
command: SWARM_ARCH=loop python -m src.cli batch benchmarks/manifests/smoke_v3_harvey.json -o results/loop_w48 -j 48
notes: Loop wins drafting tasks W13 failed (+20..+73pp); loses compare/extract (-35..-54pp). Funnel on sanity24: NOT_EXTRACTED 57.8% modal death stage.

purpose: Dual-lens extraction (guided+inventory every read) vs v1
command: SWARM_ARCH=loop python -m src.cli batch benchmarks/manifests/smoke_v3_harvey.json -o results/loop_w48_v2 -j 48
notes: Extract/compare tasks jump (+17..+61pp) but drafting tasks drown in claim flood (LPA s20 -50pp, 121 unbound). Fix: depth as controller decision (5fc3fa1).

purpose: Blackboard rebuild (v4), contract plane (v5), contract scope fix (v6) — sequential 48-task runs
command: SWARM_ARCH=loop ... -o results/loop_w48_v{4,5,6}
notes: Monotonic macro regression from contract machinery. Contract config-gated off (LOOP_CONTRACT, default 0).

purpose: NOISE FLOOR: identical v3 config re-run on same 48 tasks
command: LOOP_REFRAME_ITERATIONS= SWARM_ARCH=loop ... -o results/loop_w48_v3r2
notes: Macro is reproducible (0.3pp); class slices (n<=10) swing +-6-7pp and single tasks +-24pp on identical code. Class-level and single-task deltas are NOT evidence. Macro and criteria-mass aggregates are.

purpose: Force-analysis gate: revert v7/v8 changes to v3 baseline + prevent premature waiving of unanalyzed critical/high targets
command: SWARM_ARCH=loop python -m src.cli batch benchmarks/manifests/sanity_harvey.json -o results/loop_w48_v9 -j 48
notes: 65.4% on 44 scored tasks. Below v3 (65.6%) â€” force-analysis gate didn't help. Gate adds complexity without improving outcome.

purpose: Synthesis supplementary evidence from waived targets + claim dedup - address synthesis information loss
command: SWARM_ARCH=loop python -m src.cli batch benchmarks/manifests/smoke_v3_harvey.json -o results/loop_w48_v10 -j 48
notes: BEST FLASH-LITE RESULT. 69.7% macro. Supplementary evidence from waived targets + dedup solved synthesis information loss. Current baseline for all provenance experiments.

purpose: Gap-focused repair: term audit identifies dropped claims for targeted repair pass
command: SWARM_ARCH=loop python -m src.cli batch benchmarks/manifests/smoke_v3_harvey.json -o results/loop_w48_v11 -j 48
notes: FAILED EXPERIMENT. Gap report (term audit → targeted repair) added noise. Repair LLM confused by extra gap report section. Reverted for v12. Lesson: adding more information to repair prompt hurts; the repair pass works best with its existing packet evidence.

purpose: Raise synthesis evidence caps: content 700, evidence 350, packet 700K, repair 500K
command: SWARM_ARCH=loop python -m src.cli batch benchmarks/manifests/smoke_v3_harvey.json -o results/loop_w48_v12 -j 48
notes: FAILED EXPERIMENT. Raising evidence caps causes attention dilution. 31/48 regressed. Worst: 76.8%→21.4% on antitrust markup task. More evidence in prompt ≠ better synthesis. Reverted to v10 caps.

purpose: Render contract: compact must-include term ledger before synthesis evidence
command: SWARM_ARCH=loop python -m src.cli batch benchmarks/manifests/smoke_v3_harvey.json -o results/loop_w48_v13 -j 48
notes: REVERTED. Render contract regressed -5.5pp from v10. Third consecutive intervention (v11 gap report, v12 raised caps, v13 render contract) that failed to improve on v10. Adding structured info before evidence causes attention dilution similar to v12.

purpose: Binding gate: bind to resolved targets + pre-synthesis orphan sweep
command: SWARM_ARCH=loop python -m src.cli batch benchmarks/manifests/smoke_v3_harvey.json -o results/loop_w48_v14 -j 48
notes: Neutral. Pre-synth bind effective for complex tasks (63/150 bound on worst task) but minimal on most (0-7 bound). Orphan claims are genuinely unrelated to targets, not routing failures. Merged-waiver blind spot confirmed as false lead. 4th consecutive intervention that failed to improve on v10.

purpose: Tighter repair acceptance: 60%->90% draft length floor to reduce destructive repairs
command: SWARM_ARCH=loop python -m src.cli batch benchmarks/manifests/smoke_v3_harvey.json -o results/loop_w48_v15 -j 48
notes: Fifth consecutive failure to improve over v10 (69.7%). Repair tightening slightly regressed. 1 task (capital-markets/draft-underwriting-agreement) produced no output. The 70% ceiling is confirmed structural.

purpose: Extraction-time target binding: claims bound to targets during guided reads
command: SWARM_ARCH=loop python -m src.cli batch benchmarks/manifests/smoke_v3_harvey.json -o results/loop_w48_v16 -j 48
notes: Extraction-time binding helped bottom tasks (+10-15pp on 5 forensic targets) but hurt top tasks (-10-17pp on 8 tasks). Complex output schema likely degrades cheap model extraction quality. Net criteria delta: -14 on 46 common tasks. The approach is correct but implementation is too invasive for the extraction prompt.

purpose: Auto-bind guided-read claims to dispatching targets without prompt changes
command: SWARM_ARCH=loop python -m src.cli batch benchmarks/manifests/smoke_v3_harvey.json -o results/loop_w48_v17 -j 48
notes: FAILED. Auto-binding all guided-read claims to dispatching targets dilutes synthesis. 30 regressions vs 15 improvements, net -93 criteria. Over-binding is the problem: many extracted facts are incidentally present in read chunks but irrelevant to the dispatching targets. v16 LLM binding was selective; v17 blanket binding is worse. Must revert to v10 baseline.

purpose: auto-bind sweep + all prompt truncation caps removed (full board visibility)
command: SWARM_ARCH=loop python -m src.cli batch benchmarks/manifests/smoke_v3_harvey.json -o results/loop_w48_v18 -j 48
notes: auto-bind still hurts (-2.0pp vs v10). cap removal helped +1.7pp vs v17 (auto-bind only). next: cap removal only, no auto-bind.

purpose: cap removal only (no auto-bind) — isolate cap removal effect
command: SWARM_ARCH=loop python -m src.cli batch benchmarks/manifests/smoke_v3_harvey.json -o results/loop_w48_v19 -j 48
notes: Cap removal alone: 67.3% (-2.4pp vs v10). Neither auto-bind nor cap removal independently improve v10. v10 remains best at 69.7%.

purpose: flash/opus-4.8 alternating smart caller + no prompt truncation caps
command: SWARM_ARCH=loop SWARM_AUDIT_MODEL=claude-opus-4-8 python -m src.cli batch benchmarks/manifests/smoke_v3_harvey.json -o results/loop_w48_v20 -j 48
notes: First improvement over v10! +2.2pp. Opus alternation in smart caller (controller/maintenance/synthesis). Opus provides better strategic reasoning, mass closures, fewer stutter loops, tighter target management.

purpose: controller prompt discipline rules + periodic opus blackboard audit (AUDIT_EVERY=5)
command: SWARM_ARCH=loop SWARM_AUDIT_MODEL=claude-opus-4-8 python -m src.cli batch
notes: Regression. Prompt discipline rules distorted flash behavior — 33/48 regressed vs v20. Tasks hit max_iterations far more often (20 at iter12 vs 13 in v20). Rules cargo-cult opus behavior without reasoning capacity.

purpose: Deep opus audit (4-phase) + clean flash controller (no efficiency discipline).
command: SWARM_ARCH=loop SWARM_AUDIT_MODEL=claude-opus-4-8 python -m src.cli batch benchmarks/manifests/smoke_v3_harvey.json -o results/loop_w48_v22 -j 24
notes: 67.9% on 44 scored tasks (4 scenario tasks missing scores). Below v20 (71.9%) and v10 (69.7%). Deep opus audit didn't help enough.

purpose: Diagnostic: opus everywhere (worker + synthesis). Tests if the loop architecture is the ceiling, not model quality.
command: SWARM_ARCH=loop SWARM_WORKER_MODEL=claude-opus-4-8 SWARM_SYNTHESIS_MODEL=claude-opus-4-8 python -m src.cli batch benchmarks/manifests/smoke_v3_harvey.json -o results/loop_w48_v23_opus_all -j 12
notes: 87.7% on 22 scored tasks (partial: 22 unscored with output, 3 no output). Structure is NOT the ceiling â€” opus everywhere massively outperforms flash (87.7% vs 69.7%). Model quality is the dominant factor. ~18pp headroom for flash to close via architecture improvements.

purpose: Provenance architecture MVP: blackboard as index, claims carry source_span, analyze gets hydrated with full source text via recursive support-ref resolution. No caps on hydration. Feature flag LOOP_ANALYZE_HYDRATE=1.
command: SWARM_ARCH=loop LOOP_ANALYZE_HYDRATE=1 SWARM_WORKER_MODEL=gemini-3.1-flash-lite SWARM_SYNTHESIS_MODEL=gemini-3.5-flash python -m src.cli batch benchmarks/manifests/smoke_v3_harvey.json -o results/loop_w48_v24_hydrate -j 4
notes: -5.2pp vs v10 baseline (69.7%). Root cause: flash-lite evidence quotes are paraphrased/truncated, so _find_quote_span fails 55% of the time. Only 864 chars avg hydrated per analyze call. Architecture is correct but span matching is the bottleneck. Fix: use chunk offset ranges directly (claims already know which chunk they came from) instead of quote-based span matching.

purpose: Chunk-fallback spans: when quote matching fails, fall back to full chunk range. v24 showed 55% span miss rate; this ensures every source-backed claim has a span for hydration.
command: SWARM_ARCH=loop LOOP_ANALYZE_HYDRATE=1 SWARM_WORKER_MODEL=gemini-3.1-flash-lite SWARM_SYNTHESIS_MODEL=gemini-3.5-flash python -m src.cli batch benchmarks/manifests/smoke_v3_harvey.json -o results/loop_w48_v25_chunk_fallback -j 4
notes: Near-baseline: -1.0pp vs v10 (69.7%). Chunk-fallback recovered +4.2pp vs v24 (64.5%). 99.6% span resolution, 82x more source text per hydration (70K vs 864 chars). Pattern: reasoning tasks (identify/compare/analyze) gain up to +21.7pp; drafting/extraction tasks lose up to -29.6pp â€” chunk-fallback floods flash-lite with too much context. Next: fix content-first dedup bug (Codex finding #1), then window-based hydration instead of full-chunk dump.

purpose: Narrow hydration: section-aware 3K fallback spans instead of full 40K chunks, Â±500 window expansion, removed content-first pre-dedup bug. Should reduce avg hydration from 70K to ~5-10K chars.
command: SWARM_ARCH=loop LOOP_ANALYZE_HYDRATE=1 SWARM_WORKER_MODEL=gemini-3.1-flash-lite SWARM_SYNTHESIS_MODEL=gemini-3.5-flash python -m src.cli batch benchmarks/manifests/smoke_v3_harvey.json -o results/loop_w48_v26_narrow_hydrate -j 4
notes: Running. Three fixes: (1) removed content-first pre-dedup that dropped same-content claims from different sections, (2) chunk-fallback now uses 3K section-aware window instead of full chunk, (3) all spans get Â±500 expansion. Hypothesis: reducing context flooding should recover the 25 tasks that regressed in v25 while preserving the 18 that improved.

purpose: Provenance MVP v26: narrow 3K section-aware fallback hydration (down from v25 full-chunk 70K). Content-first dedup removed. Window expansion ±500 chars.
command: SWARM_ARCH=loop LOOP_ANALYZE_HYDRATE=1 SWARM_WORKER_MODEL=gemini-3.1-flash-lite SWARM_SYNTHESIS_MODEL=gemini-3.5-flash python -m src.cli run benchmarks/manifests/smoke_v3_harvey.json -o results/loop_w48_v26_narrow_hydrate -j 4
notes: Recovered to v10 parity (69.7%) from v24 (64.5%) and v25 (68.7%). Narrow 3K fallback reduced avg hydration from 70K to 18K chars. 20 tasks improved, 23 regressed — true wash. Improvements concentrate on extraction/comparison tasks. Provenance architecture is now neutral on flash-lite, ready for next iteration. User directive: keep provenance arch even if temporarily worse.

purpose: Provenance v27: synthesis hydration — source text in synthesis + repair prompts, capped at 400K chars. Both analyze and synthesis hydration enabled.
command: SWARM_ARCH=loop LOOP_ANALYZE_HYDRATE=1 LOOP_SYNTHESIS_HYDRATE=1 SWARM_WORKER_MODEL=gemini-3.1-flash-lite SWARM_SYNTHESIS_MODEL=gemini-3.5-flash python -m src.cli batch benchmarks/manifests/smoke_v3_harvey.json -o results/loop_w48_v27_synth_hydrate -j 4
notes: Synthesis hydration breakthrough: +5.0pp over v10 baseline. 32/48 tasks improved. Largest single-version gain in project history. Both analyze + synthesis hydration enabled. 2 tasks required rerun (output write crash, fixed). Codex-validated design: packets decide content, source text for precision.

purpose: TF-IDF centered 3K fallback spans: when exact/normalized quote matching fails, use TF-IDF term scoring to find the best 600-char window, then pad to 3K centered on it. Same window size as v27 fallback, but centered on matching text instead of section heading.
command: SWARM_ARCH=loop LOOP_ANALYZE_HYDRATE=1 LOOP_SYNTHESIS_HYDRATE=1 SWARM_WORKER_MODEL=gemini-3.1-flash-lite SWARM_SYNTHESIS_MODEL=gemini-3.5-flash python -m src.cli batch benchmarks/manifests/smoke_v3_harvey.json -o results/loop_w48_v28c_tfidf_pad1200 -j 48
notes: TF-IDF centering +1.9pp over v27. Three padding variants tested: pad=80 (73.2%), pad=500 (72.7%), pad=1200 (76.8%). Key insight: centering helps but window size must stay at 3K. Also tested: opus extracts 3.1x more claims/task (1113 vs 355) — extraction depth is the primary bottleneck, not span quality.

purpose: Extraction depth: 20K chunks (from 40K), 32K output tokens (from 16K), completeness pass. Target: close 3.1x extraction gap vs opus.
command: SWARM_ARCH=loop LOOP_ANALYZE_HYDRATE=1 LOOP_SYNTHESIS_HYDRATE=1 LOOP_READ_COMPLETENESS=1 python -m src.cli batch benchmarks/manifests/smoke_v3_harvey.json -o results/loop_w48_v29_depth -j 48
notes: Claims 334->801/task (2.4x, vs opus 1113). 25 improved, 12 regressed. Big wins: fintech +30pp, PERM +19pp (97%), protective order +16pp. Regressions: accounting -17pp, LPA scenarios -16pp, reg-stmt -10pp. Regressions may be synthesis overwhelmed by claim volume. Next: investigate regressions, possibly packet selection diversity.

purpose: Opus 4.8 synthesis with flash-lite extraction + flash smart tier
config_path: env: SWARM_WORKER_MODEL=gemini-3.1-flash-lite, SWARM_SMART_MODEL=gemini-3.5-flash, SWARM_SYNTHESIS_MODEL=claude-opus-4-8, LOOP_READ_CHUNK_CHARS=20000, LOOP_READ_COMPLETENESS=1, LOOP_ANALYZE_HYDRATE=1, LOOP_SYNTHESIS_HYDRATE=1
command: python -m src.cli batch benchmarks/manifests/smoke_v3_harvey.json -o results/loop_w48_v29c_opus_synth -j 48

metrics.failure_reason: Anthropic API credit balance exhausted mid-run

notes: MASSIVE result: +9.6pp over v29 on 34/48 tasks. 14 failed due to Anthropic credit exhaustion. Opus synthesis lifts nearly every task. Need credits to complete remaining 14.

purpose: GPT-5.5 synthesis with flash-lite extraction + flash smart tier. Full 48-task run.
config_path: env: SWARM_WORKER_MODEL=gemini-3.1-flash-lite, SWARM_SMART_MODEL=gemini-3.5-flash, SWARM_SYNTHESIS_MODEL=gpt-5.5, LOOP_READ_CHUNK_CHARS=20000, LOOP_READ_COMPLETENESS=1, LOOP_ANALYZE_HYDRATE=1, LOOP_SYNTHESIS_HYDRATE=1
command: SWARM_ARCH=loop SWARM_SYNTHESIS_MODEL=gpt-5.5 LOOP_ANALYZE_HYDRATE=1 LOOP_SYNTHESIS_HYDRATE=1 LOOP_READ_COMPLETENESS=1 python -m src.cli batch benchmarks/manifests/smoke_v3_harvey.json -o results/loop_w48_v29d_gpt55_synth -j 48
notes: GPT-5.5 synthesis matches opus quality (88.8% vs 88.9% on 34-task overlap) on all 48 tasks with zero failures. 44/48 improved over v29 flash synthesis. Cost: .51/task avg. Proves synthesis model quality is the dominant lever: flash-lite extraction + flash control + premium synthesis = 88.8% vs 79.1% (flash synthesis) or 69.7% (v10 baseline). Only 3 minor regressions.
